title: Fix missing border on Library button in Discover header
author: 4arjun

evidence.lived_experience: none found
evidence.organizational_memory: none found
evidence.uncertainty: none found
evidence.causal_reasoning: When a category page loads, getRootTaxonomyCss() applies inline styles to the header including `--color: 0 0 0`. However, the border-border Tailwind class resolves to `var(--color-border)`, which is defined in the `@theme` block as `rgb(var(--color) / var(--border-alpha))`. The issue is that `--color-border` is computed at the theme level and doesn't automatically re-evaluate when `--color` is overridden inline on a descendant element.
evidence.cognitive_questions: none found

title: Remove pre-submit tax UI assertions from checkout tests
author: gumclaw

evidence.lived_experience: Tax-related checkout tests... are the biggest source of CI flakiness by volume. Tests assert exact tax/total amounts inside `check_out` blocks **before** clicking "Pay". Tax amounts are computed asynchronously after form fill, so assertions often run before the async response arrives.
evidence.organizational_memory: Same pattern as #3822 and #3823: remove all remaining pre-submit UI assertions from `check_out` blocks and rely on deterministic post-purchase database assertions. References to the VCR/JS conflict where `setup_js` turns off VCR but the shipping hook turns it back on with WebMock still allowing net connections.
evidence.uncertainty: None. Author asserts clear direction throughout. Phrase 'continue to verify tax correctness' shows confidence rather than doubt.
evidence.causal_reasoning: Tax amounts are computed asynchronously after form fill, so assertions often run before the async response arrives. Explains the root cause (race condition between async computation and synchronous assertion) and the grounded solution (move assertions post-purchase where determinism is guaranteed).
evidence.cognitive_questions: None found. No genuine questions like 'Should we also...?' or 'What if...?'

This was primarily happening on Hetzner runners. — Strong causal chain grounded in system behavior.
